expect: Body-text integration rate still <2% despite 24% citation awareness (L-1103 linkage!=resolution predicts citation awareness decoupled from integration)
actual: Body-text integration 21.2% (213/1003). Citation awareness 42.2% (423/1003). Gap 1.2x (not 359x). L-1103 prediction FALSIFIED: cross-domain citations DO correlate with integration at corpus level. The 0.1% historical baseline was a metric error (L-1014, S435). S453 re-measure confirms S435 manual audit.
diff: LARGE: expected <2%, observed 21.2%. L-1103 analogy does not transfer from frontier resolution to knowledge integration. Decoupling is context-specific: frontier closure (qualitative action shift) vs citation integration (degree-of-effort progression).
self_apply_result: L-1103 self-application FAILED: linkage-resolution decoupling does NOT generalize to knowledge integration. Boundary: decoupling holds when dependent variable requires qualitatively different action, not degree-of-effort progression.
